raw:
SCENE ANALYSIS - A wooden table holds a yellow napkin, a green cloth, a red can, a yellow can, a tomato sauce can, and a light blue brush. The task is to move the croissant (not visible in the image, so assume it's on the table) to above the towel (yellow napkin). STRATEGY - First, reach for the croissant, grasp it, then transport it to the towel's position, and place it above. Failure modes include missing the croissant or dropping it. AFFORDANCE REASONING - Step 1 reach the croissant (assumed on the table) at its body; Step 2 grasp its body for hold; Step 3 transport to towel's area; Step 4 place above the towel.
Goal: on(croissant, towel); stable(croissant)
Scene: yellow_napkin, green_cloth, red_can, yellow_can, tomato_sauce_can, light_blue_brush, table
Step 1: reach(croissant)
  affordance_region: the body of the croissant (assumed on the table)
  contact: gripper_state(open) [progress]
  spatial: distance(gripper, croissant, <, 0.03) [completion]
  direction: approach_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: distance(gripper, croissant) < 0.03
Step 2: grasp(croissant)
  affordance_region: the body of the croissant
  contact: holding(croissant) [completion]
  direction: grasp_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: holding(croissant)
Step 3: transport(croissant -> towel)
  affordance_region: an empty area above the yellow_napkin
  spatial: above(croissant, yellow_napkin, 0.05) [completion]
  safety: no_drop(croissant)
  done_when: above(croissant, yellow_napkin, 0.05)
Step 4: place(croissant -> towel)
  affordance_region: an empty area above the yellow_napkin
  contact: surface_contact(croissant, yellow_napkin) [completion]; released(croissant) [completion]
  pose: stable(croissant) [completion]
  done_when: surface_contact(croissant, yellow_napkin) AND released(croissant)
<END_OF_PLAN>

steps.affordance_region: the body of the croissant (assumed on the table)
an empty area above the yellow_napkin
an empty area above the yellow_napkin
